resources.string: Notifies you whenever an app requests a Google Play Integrity verdict. Scope it to Google Play Store.
Alerts when a watched app uses the Play Integrity API.
%1$s (%2$s) just asked Google Play for a Play Integrity verdict.
Watching Google Play Store ✓ — last Integrity request seen %1$s.
Module loaded ✓ — make sure Google Play Store is ticked in the module’s scope, then restart it. Status flips to “watching” after Play Store’s next Integrity request.
Module not active — enable Play Integrity Alert in LSPosed, make sure Google Play Store is ticked in its scope, then restart Play Store.

1. Enable this module in LSPosed.
2. In the module’s scope, make sure Google Play Store is ticked — LSPosed pre-selects it by default. Then restart Play Store (button below) or reboot. (You don’t need to tick this app — LSPosed scopes a module to itself automatically.)
3. Below, keep “Watch all apps” on, or turn it off and choose specific apps.
When a watched app requests a Play Integrity verdict, you’ll get a notification here.
Tap “Force stop”, then reopen Play Store so the module loads into it.
No Play Integrity usage detected yet.
